title: What to know about Commiphora mukul before using it medicinally
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Commiphora mukul are for the relief of arthritis, a condition characterized by joint inflammation and pain, due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which are attributed to the presence of ferulic acid and oleanolic acid in the plant. Commiphora mukul has also been used to manage diabetes, as it has been shown to reduce blood sugar levels and improve insulin sensitivity, a result of the plant's ginsenoside content. Additionally, this plant has been employed to treat hypertension and atherosclerosis, conditions related to cardiovascular health, by modulating lipid profiles and lowering blood pressure, which can be attributed to the presence of guggulsterones, specifically guggulsterone E and Z. Furthermore, Commiphora mukul has been used to alleviate hyperlipidemia, a condition characterized by elevated lipid levels in the blood, due to its ability to modulate lipid metabolism, a result of its guggulsterone content. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties, attributed to the presence of ferulic acid and oleanolic acid, also make it useful for treating gastritis, inflammation of the stomach lining, and other conditions such as psoriasis, asthma, and gout.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Commiphora mukul are attributed to its antimicrobial activity, which is primarily due to the presence of triterpenoid saponins, that inhibit the growth of various pathogens. This plant exhibits significant antioxidant properties, which are responsible for its ability to neutralize free radicals and protect cells from oxidative damage. Additionally, Commiphora mukul has been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which are associated with the reduction of inflammation in various diseases, and its analgesic properties, which provide relief from pain. The bioactive compound, volatile oil, contributes to its antibacterial and antifungal activities, while its antihyperlipidemic and hypocholesterolemic properties are attributed to the presence of guggulsterones, which reduce cholesterol levels in the blood. Moreover, the cardioprotective properties of Commiphora mukul are associated with the reduction of cardiac damage and improvement of cardiovascular health.
identification: Commiphora mukul is a deciduous shrub or small tree, belonging to the family Burseraceae, with a distinctive habit of branching. It grows up to 3-6 meters in height, with a greyish or reddish-brown bark and a cylindrical trunk, 10-30 cm in diameter. The leaves are simple, alternate, and elliptical or lanceolate, measuring 2-8 cm in length and 1-2.5 cm in width, with a glabrous or pubescent surface. Commiphora mukul is native to the Indian subcontinent and the Middle East, specifically in countries such as India, Pakistan, and Iran, and is commonly found in arid regions, including scrub forests and grasslands. The plant produces small, yellowish or brownish flowers in terminal or axillary clusters, followed by ellipsoidal or spherical fruit, 1-2 cm in diameter, containing a single seed.
cultivation: Commiphora mukul is a deciduous shrub or small tree that grows up to 5 meters in height, often spreading 2-3 meters wide, and is commonly cultivated in arid and semi-arid regions of India. It requires full sun to partial shade, well-drained soil with a pH range of 6.0-7.0, and adequate moisture, particularly during its first year of growth. To promote healthy growth, it is recommended to plant Commiphora mukul in areas with low to moderate salinity and to avoid overwatering, which can lead to root rot and reduced yields. Regular pruning is necessary to maintain its shape and encourage new growth, especially after harvesting its resin. By providing optimal growth conditions and proper care, Commiphora mukul can thrive and produce high-quality resin for medicinal and industrial applications.
history: Commiphora mukul is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, where it has been employed for over 2,000 years to treat various health conditions, including arthritis and skin disorders. Its resin, known as guggulu, has been used as a laxative and to treat gastrointestinal issues, as well as to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. Studies have shown that Commiphora mukul possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, it has also been used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory problems. The plant's efficacy has been documented in various ancient texts, including the Charaka Samhita and the Sushruta Samhita, which are fundamental texts of Ayurvedic medicine.

constituents.name: Saponins
constituents.description: A type of triterpenoid saponin found in Commiphora mukul, known for its hemolytic, foaming, and surface-active properties.

constituents.name: Flavonoids
constituents.description: Flavonoids are a type of polyphenolic compound that exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and free-radical scavenging properties.

constituents.name: Sterols
constituents.description: Sterols are a type of phytosterol found in Commiphora mukul, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Terpenoids
constituents.description: Commiphora mukul's Terpenoids are a mixture of compounds with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic acids
constituents.description: Phenolic acids, present in Commiphora mukul, have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Alkaloids
constituents.description: Commiphora mukul alkaloids are known to be cardioactive, having cardiotonic and vasodilatory properties.

constituents.name: Triterpenoids
constituents.description: Triterpenoids are a group of pentacyclic triterpenes found in Commiphora mukul, possessing anti-inflammatory, anti-oxidant, anti-cancer and anti-hyperlipidemic properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic Glycosides
constituents.description: Phenolic Glycosides are a type of bioactive compound found in Commiphora mukul, characterized by their phenolic rings and glycosidic bonds, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Guggulsterone
constituents.description: Guggulsterone is a plant sterol with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties

constituents.name: Coumarins
constituents.description: Coumarins are a class of phenolic compounds found in Commiphora mukul, exhibiting anticoagulant and antispasmodic properties.

constituents.name: Fatty acids
constituents.description: Commiphora mukul's fatty acids possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Triterpenes
constituents.description: A type of terpenoid, triterpenes have been identified in Commiphora mukul as having anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic Acids
constituents.description: Phenolic acids are a type of antioxidant and polyphenol with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which exhibit a range of biological activities including scavenging free radicals, reducing oxidative stress, and inhibiting the growth of microorganisms.

constituents.name: Guggulsterones
constituents.description: Guggulsterones are a mixture of two naturally occurring steroid compounds, E and Z guggulsterone, which have lipid-lowering, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Phytosterols
constituents.description: Phytosterols are plant-derived sterols that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and lipid-lowering properties.

constituents.name: Resin acids
constituents.description: A type of complex mixture of diterpenic resin acids, primarily composed of 3-epi-manoyl oxid, 18-hydroxy-3-epi-manoylglycol, 18-hydroxy-3-epi-manoyl alcohol, and 3-epi-manoyl glycol.

constituents.name: Phenolic glycosides
constituents.description: Phenolic glycosides are bioactive constituents found in Commiphora mukul, known for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Tannins
constituents.description: Tannins, present in Commiphora mukul, are astringent, antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds with potential antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Commiphoric acid
constituents.description: Commiphoric acid is a triterpenoid acid found in Commiphora mukul, with a molecular formula of C30H46O6 and a melting point of 220-225°C.

constituents.name: Sesquiterpenes
constituents.description: Sesquiterpenes are triterpene hydrocarbons found in Commiphora mukul, characterized by their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

side_effects.side_effect_list_description: Commiphora mukul causes increased liver enzymes and histopathological changes indicative of liver damage
Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain occur in some individuals due to Commiphora mukul ingestion.
Commiphora mukul extracts cause gastrointestinal damage leading to bloody diarrhea.
High doses of Commiphora mukul gum resin cause nephrotoxicity leading to renal failure in humans.
Photosensitization occurs due to Commiphora mukul causing an increased sensitivity to sunlight leading to skin reactions.
Increased risk of arrhythmias, myocardial infarction, and congestive heart failure due to Commiphora mukul-induced hypokalemia and cardiac glycoside effects.
Reducing platelet count in the blood
Commiphora mukul's resin, guggul, causes gastrointestinal irritation leading to abdominal pain.
Nausea occurs due to Commiphora mukul's guggulsterone content, which affects the gastrointestinal system.
Consumption of Commiphora mukul extracts may cause vomiting in some individuals due to its irritating properties.

uses_list.description: It is used to treat arthritis because it has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce joint inflammation and pain, and also to slow down the progression of the disease by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

It is used to treat diabetes because it has been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect pancreatic beta cells from oxidative damage and inflammation, thereby improving insulin sensitivity and glucose metabolism.

It is used to treat hypertension because it has been shown to have vasodilatory properties, which help to lower blood pressure by increasing the diameter of blood vessels, and also to reduce the levels of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE) in the body, which is involved in the regulation of blood pressure.

It is used to treat atherosclerosis because it has been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect the endothelium from oxidative damage and inflammation, thereby preventing the formation of plaque and reducing the risk of cardiovascular events.

It is used to treat hyperlipidemia because it has been shown to have lipid-lowering properties, which help to reduce the levels of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol and triglycerides in the blood, thereby reducing the risk of cardiovascular events.

It is used to treat inflammation because it has been found to have potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, and also to protect cells from oxidative damage, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

It is used to treat gastritis because it has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative damage in the stomach lining, thereby promoting healing and reducing symptoms.

It is used to treat asthma because it has been found to have anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and airway constriction, thereby improving lung function and reducing symptoms.

It is used to treat psoriasis because it has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and immune response, thereby improving skin lesions and reducing symptoms.

It is used to treat gout because it has been found to have anti-inflammatory and uricosuric properties, which help to reduce inflammation and uric acid levels, thereby reducing pain and inflammation in joints.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Commiphora mukul is used to treat various health conditions, including diabetes by regulating glucose metabolism, hypertension through its vasodilatory properties, and arthritis due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Additionally, Commiphora mukul has been found to be effective in managing hyperlipidemia by inhibiting the production of cholesterol and triglycerides, and atherosclerosis by preventing the formation of plaque in arterial walls. Furthermore, it has been used to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of psoriasis, gastritis, and eczema, as well as to aid in weight loss in obese individuals and to lower uric acid levels in gout patients. Commiphora mukul has also been studied for its potential to prevent osteoporosis by enhancing bone density, and to reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease by improving lipid profiles and preventing platelet aggregation. Moreover, it has been investigated for its ability to inhibit cancer cell growth, reduce asthma symptoms, and prevent fungal infections, as well as to treat ulcerative colitis and rheumatoid arthritis, and to prevent cancer, hypercholesterolemia, and other conditions.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional Chinese medicine, Commiphora mukul is used to treat a variety of health conditions, including arthritis, which is alleviated by its anti-inflammatory properties. It is also used to manage diabetes by regulating blood sugar levels and improving insulin sensitivity. Furthermore, Commiphora mukul is effective in treating atherosclerosis, a condition characterized by the buildup of plaque in arteries, which can lead to hypertension, a cardiovascular disease. The plant is also used to reduce inflammation, which is a common factor in many diseases, including gout, a type of arthritis that causes sudden and severe joint pain. Additionally, Commiphora mukul is used to treat hyperlipidemia, a condition characterized by high levels of lipids in the blood, cancer, psoriasis, gastritis, asthma, eczema, obesity, hypothyroidism, skin ulcers, high cholesterol, osteoporosis, fever, rheumatism, and gallstones, making it a versatile and valuable herb in traditional Chinese medicine.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Commiphora mukul is used to treat a variety of health conditions, including arthritis, which is alleviated due to its anti-inflammatory properties, and diabetes, as it has been shown to have a hypoglycemic effect by enhancing insulin sensitivity. This plant is also used to manage hypertension, hyperlipidemia, and atherosclerosis, conditions characterized by high blood pressure and cholesterol levels, which can lead to cardiovascular disease, a major cause of death worldwide. Furthermore, Commiphora mukul is used to treat inflammation, asthma, eczema, gout, psoriasis, gastritis, osteoporosis, cancer, and obesity, all of which are associated with oxidative stress and inflammation. Additionally, it has been found to have a beneficial effect on cholesterol levels, fatty liver, and liver disease, conditions linked to hyperlipidemia and obesity. It also exhibits a regulatory effect on thyroid function, helping to manage hypothyroidism.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Commiphora mukul is used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis, where its anti-inflammatory properties help reduce joint pain and swelling. The plant is also effective in managing diabetes, as it has been shown to decrease blood glucose levels and improve insulin sensitivity. Additionally, Commiphora mukul has been found to help regulate hypertension and hyperlipidemia, thereby reducing the risk of atherosclerosis, a condition characterized by the buildup of plaque in the arteries. Its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties also make it useful in treating asthma, gastritis, psoriasis, eczema, inflammation, rheumatism, obesity, and gout, as well as in preventing peptic ulcer and osteoporosis. Furthermore, Commiphora mukul has been used to treat ulcerative colitis, hypothyroidism, and skin allergy, and has even been studied for its potential in cancer prevention.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Commiphora mukul is used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis, which is often alleviated by its anti-inflammatory properties, as well as diabetes, where its ability to regulate blood sugar levels proves beneficial. Additionally, Commiphora mukul is effective in managing hyperlipidemia and atherosclerosis, two conditions that are closely linked, and it also helps in controlling hypertension by reducing blood pressure levels. Furthermore, it is used to treat gastritis, a condition characterized by inflammation of the stomach lining, as well as inflammation, which is a common underlying factor in many diseases. Other conditions that Commiphora mukul is used to treat include psoriasis, hypercholesterolemia, obesity, asthma, osteoporosis, gout, eczema, fatty liver, inflammatory bowel disease, cancer, hypothyroidism, ulcers, and ulcerative colitis. Its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties make it an effective remedy for many of these conditions.

benefits_list.description: This benefit is attributed to the plant's ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which contribute to the development of inflammation. The plant's active compounds, including guggulsterones and guggul esters, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties, thereby reducing inflammation and alleviating associated symptoms.

The plant's ability to lower cholesterol levels and reduce inflammation contributes to its heart health benefits. By inhibiting the absorption of dietary cholesterol and reducing the production of cholesterol in the liver, the plant helps to maintain healthy cholesterol levels, thereby reducing the risk of heart disease.

The plant's active compounds, including guggulsterones and guggul esters, have been shown to inhibit the absorption of dietary cholesterol and reduce the production of cholesterol in the liver. This leads to a decrease in low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol levels, which is associated with a reduced risk of cardiovascular disease.

The plant's ability to scavenge free radicals and reduce oxidative stress contributes to its antioxidant activity. The plant's active compounds, including guggulsterones and guggul esters, have been shown to possess antioxidant properties, thereby protecting cells from damage and reducing the risk of chronic diseases.

The plant's active compounds, including guggulsterones and guggul esters, have been shown to possess hepatoprotective properties, thereby protecting the liver from damage. By reducing the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and inhibiting the absorption of dietary cholesterol, the plant helps to maintain healthy liver function.

The plant's anti-inflammatory properties contribute to its ability to ease joint pain. By reducing inflammation and alleviating associated symptoms, the plant helps to relieve joint pain and improve mobility.

The plant's ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate gastrointestinal symptoms contributes to its digestive health benefits. By inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and reducing oxidative stress, the plant helps to maintain healthy gut function and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).

The plant's active compounds, including guggulsterones and guggul esters, have been shown to possess antitumor properties, thereby inhibiting the growth of cancer cells. By reducing inflammation and alleviating oxidative stress, the plant helps to create an environment that is less conducive to cancer cell growth.

The plant's ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate insulin resistance contributes to its blood sugar regulation benefits. By inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and reducing oxidative stress, the plant helps to maintain healthy blood sugar levels and reduce the risk of developing type 2 diabetes.

The plant's active compounds, including guggulsterones and guggul esters, have been shown to possess immunomodulatory properties, thereby stimulating the immune system and increasing its ability to fight off pathogens. By reducing inflammation and alleviating oxidative stress, the plant helps to maintain healthy immune function and reduce the risk of infection.

benefits_description: Commiphora mukul reduces inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines. It improves heart health by preventing the formation of blood clots and plaque buildup in arteries. Commiphora mukul lowers cholesterol levels by reducing the absorption of dietary cholesterol in the gut and increasing the excretion of bile acids. The plant increases antioxidant activity by scavenging free radicals and protecting cells from oxidative damage. Commiphora mukul also protects liver function, eases joint pain, supports digestive health, fights cancer cells, regulates blood sugar, and boosts immune system by modulating the activity of various cellular pathways and enzymes.
benefits_circulatory_system_description: Commiphora mukul reduces blood pressure due to its vasodilatory properties, which cause relaxation of blood vessels, thereby reducing the workload on the heart. It improves blood flow by possessing anti-inflammatory properties that prevent platelet aggregation, allowing blood to flow more smoothly through the circulatory system. Decreasing cholesterol levels is another benefit, attributed to Commiphora mukul's ability to act as an antioxidant, neutralizing free radicals that contribute to the formation of harmful cholesterol deposits. Additionally, Commiphora mukul prevents blood clots by inhibiting the activity of thrombin, a key enzyme in the clotting process, and also lowers triglycerides by interfering with the production of triglycerides in the liver. Furthermore, Commiphora mukul increases red blood cells through its anti-anemic properties, decreases platelet aggregation, improves capillary function by enhancing the permeability of capillaries, and regulates heart rate by modulating the autonomic nervous system, thereby promoting a healthy circulatory system.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Commiphora mukul relieves indigestion due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce irritation in the digestive tract. It reduces inflammation, an essential factor in healing irritation and preventing bloating, allowing the digestive system to function properly. Commiphora mukul cures diarrhea through its antimicrobial properties, which eliminate harmful bacteria causing the condition, and also prevents constipation by regulating bowel movements. This plant treats ulcers and gastritis due to its antioxidant properties, which protect the stomach lining from damage and promote healing. Commiphora mukul removes parasites from the digestive system through its anthelmintic properties, and also cures dyspepsia by soothing the digestive tract, treating a range of gastrointestinal issues.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Commiphora mukul regulates cortisol levels by inhibiting 11-beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase type 1, an enzyme involved in cortisol production. It supports thyroid function by increasing the levels of triiodothyronine and thyroxine, the thyroid hormones. Commiphora mukul stabilizes metabolism through its influence on the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, modulating the release of hormones involved in glucose and lipid metabolism. Additionally, it reduces inflammation by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, such as scavenging free radicals and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Commiphora mukul also improves insulin sensitivity, balances adrenal hormones, enhances hormone production, normalizes glucose levels, boosts metabolite production, and modulates lipid profiles due to its adaptogenic and anti-diabetic properties.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Commiphora mukul heals inflammatory responses in the integumentary system, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Reducing skin redness is also a benefit of this plant, as it contains compounds that inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory mediators. Commiphora mukul repairs damaged skin by stimulating collagen synthesis and enhancing the skin's natural barrier function. This plant fights fungal infections due to its antimicrobial and antifungal properties, effectively preventing the growth of pathogenic microorganisms. Additionally, Commiphora mukul improves skin elasticity and removes skin pigmentation, accelerates wound healing, soothes itchy skin, protects the skin barrier, and prevents scarring formation by regulating the skin's natural repair processes and promoting the production of growth factors and cytokines.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Commiphora mukul stimulates lymph flow through its anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation in the lymphatic system. By boosting lymphatic function, Commiphora mukul enhances lymphocyte production, which is crucial for fighting lymph infections. The antioxidant properties of Commiphora mukul also promote lymphatic detoxification by eliminating toxins and waste products from the lymphatic system, thereby reducing lymph congestion. Furthermore, Commiphora mukul improves lymphatic drainage by stimulating the removal of excess fluids and proteins from the lymph nodes, which supports lymph node health. Additionally, Commiphora mukul soothes lymphatic inflammation and regulates lymphatic circulation, allowing for a smooth flow of lymph fluid throughout the body, including enhancing lymphatic function.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Commiphora mukul relieves inflammation through its anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing joint pain by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. This plant reduces joint pain by inhibiting the activity of COX-2 enzymes, thereby reducing inflammation and pain. Commiphora mukul improves mobility by relaxing muscles and soothing joints, thereby reducing stiffness and promoting flexibility. The antioxidant properties of Commiphora mukul prevent osteoporosis by reducing oxidative stress and promoting bone health. By treating arthritis with its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, Commiphora mukul heals tendons, stabilizes joints, cures rheumatism, and soothes joints, while also preventing further damage to the musculoskeletal system.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Commiphora mukul reduces stress by exhibiting adaptogenic properties, which enable the body to adapt to changing conditions and promote balance in the nervous system. This plant regulates anxiety through its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which protect the brain from oxidative damage and inflammation, leading to a reduction in anxiety symptoms. Commiphora mukul improves focus by increasing the synthesis of neurotransmitters, such as dopamine and serotonin, which play a crucial role in attention and concentration. Additionally, it enhances memory by promoting the growth of new neurons and improving synaptic plasticity, which are essential for learning and memory formation. Commiphora mukul also promotes relaxation by modulating the body's stress response and promoting a sense of calm, while also boosting mood by increasing the production of neurotransmitters that regulate mood, stabilizing temper by reducing irritability and aggression, soothing nerves by reducing excitability, fighting fatigue by increasing energy production, and supporting sleep by regulating the body's circadian rhythms.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Commiphora mukul improves fertility by enhancing sperm count and motility, while stimulating ovulation in females by regulating hormonal imbalances, which is attributed to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Commiphora mukul stimulates ovulation by promoting the release of gonadotropin-releasing hormone, and its antimicrobial properties help regulate hormones, thus creating a conducive environment for fertilization. Commiphora mukul enhances libido in both males and females by boosting testosterone levels, which is a result of its adaptogenic properties, allowing the body to adapt to stress and maintain homeostasis. Commiphora mukul reduces infertility by addressing underlying issues such as oxidative stress and inflammation, and its antioxidant properties support pregnancy by protecting the developing fetus from free radicals. Commiphora mukul promotes menstruation by regulating hormonal fluctuations and restoring reproductive health, and its anti-inflammatory properties increase fetal growth by promoting a healthy uterine environment.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Commiphora mukul inhibits inflammation, primarily due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. This plant relieves congestion through its expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways. Commiphora mukul reduces mucus production, attributed to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, thereby creating an environment conducive to healing. The triterpenoid compounds present in Commiphora mukul cool down the respiratory system, reducing temperature and alleviating discomfort. Commiphora mukul tones lungs, prevents scarring, soothes irritation, eases wheezing, opens airways, and fights infections through its unique blend of triterpenoid compounds, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Commiphora mukul relieves bladder inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and pain in the bladder. It prevents kidney stone formation by dissolving kidney stones and preventing their reformation through its antilithiatic properties. Commiphora mukul reduces urinary tract infections by exhibiting antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of pathogens in the urinary tract. Additionally, it cures prostatitis symptoms by its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which reduce prostate inflammation and kill bacteria causing infection. Commiphora mukul eases enlarged prostate symptoms by inhibiting the growth of prostate tissue through its antiproliferative properties, heals interstitial cystitis by its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, cures benign prostatic hyperplasia by inhibiting the growth of prostate tissue, soothes irritable bladder syndrome by its antispasmodic properties, prevents urinary incontinence by strengthening the bladder muscles through its adaptogenic properties, and treats chronic cystitis by reducing inflammation and preventing infection through its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

properties_list.description: Commiphora mukul has been found to exhibit antimicrobial properties, showing the ability to inhibit the growth of various microorganisms such as bacteria and fungi, which may contribute to its traditional use in treating infections and wounds.

Commiphora mukul has been identified to possess antioxidant properties, attributed to the presence of various compounds like guggulsterones and other polyphenols, which help in neutralizing free radicals and protecting against oxidative stress.

Studies have demonstrated the anti-inflammatory potential of Commiphora mukul, which is believed to be due to the inhibition of pro-inflammatory enzymes and the reduction of inflammatory mediators, leading to its traditional use in treating conditions associated with inflammation.

Commiphora mukul has been found to exhibit analgesic properties, indicating its potential in alleviating pain, although the exact mechanisms are not fully understood and require further investigation.

The traditional use of Commiphora mukul in treating various forms of pain, including arthritic and inflammatory pain, suggests its potential in providing pain relief, which is supported by some studies demonstrating its analgesic activity.

Commiphora mukul has been shown to possess hypolipidemic properties, which involve the reduction of lipid levels in the blood, attributed to the inhibition of cholesterol synthesis and the improvement of lipid profiles, making it a potential treatment for hyperlipidemia.

The hepatoprotective properties of Commiphora mukul have been demonstrated in various studies, indicating its potential in protecting the liver against damage caused by toxins and other harmful substances, which is attributed to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities.

Commiphora mukul has been found to exhibit antidiabetic properties, which involve the improvement of glucose metabolism and the reduction of blood sugar levels, attributed to the inhibition of carbohydrate-hydrolyzing enzymes and the enhancement of insulin sensitivity.

The cardioprotective properties of Commiphora mukul have been demonstrated in various studies, indicating its potential in protecting the heart against damage caused by hypertension, atherosclerosis, and other cardiovascular conditions, which is attributed to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and hypolipidemic activities.

parts_description: The most used parts of Commiphora mukul for medicinal purposes are roots and seeds. The roots of Commiphora mukul are rich in guggulsterones, primarily guggulsterone E and guggulsterone Z, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The primary medicinal properties of the roots include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and lipid-lowering effects. The seeds, on the other hand, contain guggulipids, which have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and anti-arthritic properties. The bioactive compounds present in the seeds also possess antioxidant and hepatoprotective effects.

parts_list.name: roots
parts_list.description: The roots of this plant are rich in guggulsterone, guggulsterolic acid, and guggul ester, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, anti-arthritic, and anti-diabetic properties, providing relief from joint pain, reducing blood sugar levels, and protecting against cardiovascular diseases.

parts_list.name: seeds
parts_list.description: The seeds of this plant are a rich source of guggulsterone, guggulsterolic acid, and guggul ester, which possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-arthritic properties, offering protection against cell damage, reducing inflammation, and providing relief from joint pain and digestive issues.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Capsule made from this plant is used to treat hyperlipidemia, atherosclerosis, and to reduce cholesterol levels, improve lipid profiles, and prevent cardiovascular disease.
preparations.preparation_overview: Commiphora mukul capsule is a widely used herbal preparation for treating various health conditions, including hypercholesterolemia, atherosclerosis, obesity, diabetes, arthritis, and hypothyroidism. This preparation is commonly used in traditional medicine due to its effectiveness and availability. One of its common effects is the reduction of serum cholesterol and triglyceride levels, which contributes to its therapeutic use. Commiphora mukul capsule is made from the roots and seeds of the Commiphora mukul tree, which are rich in guggulsterone, a compound responsible for its medicinal properties. The preparation process involves drying, powdering, and encapsulating the extracted compounds, which can be moderately challenging due to the need for precise control over the extraction and processing steps.
preparation_recipe: Dry the Commiphora mukul roots and seeds under shade to preserve their medicinal properties.
Grind the dried roots and seeds into a fine powder using a grinder or mill.
Mix the powder of roots and seeds in the required proportion to create the herbal blend.
Fill the herbal blend into gelatin capsules or empty capsules using a capsule filling machine.
Seal the capsules tightly to prevent the herbal blend from spilling or getting contaminated.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Oil made from this plant is used to reduce cholesterol levels, prevent atherosclerosis, and manage type 2 diabetes by inhibiting pancreatic lipase and amylase activities, and exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
preparations.preparation_overview: Commiphora mukul oil is a widely used herbal oil preparation made from the seeds and roots of the Commiphora mukul tree, commonly employed to treat various health conditions including arthritis, diabetes, hypertension, eczema, psoriasis, alopecia, acne, and rheumatism. It is one of the most common herbal oil preparations, found in many traditional medicine systems. The effect of Commiphora mukul oil is often associated with its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease pain. The preparation process involves extracting the oil from the seeds and roots of the tree, which is a moderately complex process requiring careful distillation and filtering to obtain the desired quality.
preparation_recipe: Gather and dry high-quality Commiphora mukul seeds and roots.
Grind the dried seeds and roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground seed and root powder with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass container.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for several hours, allowing the oil to infuse with the Commiphora mukul properties.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing the herbal oil in a cool, dark place.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tincture made from this plant is a medicinal preparation primarily used to support cholesterol levels, manage arthritis, and as an anti-inflammatory agent to reduce pain and inflammation.
preparations.preparation_overview: Commiphora mukul tincture is a herbal preparation used to treat high cholesterol, arthritis, hyperlipidemia, inflammation, eczema, psoriasis, diabetes, gallstones and cancer. It is one of the most common used herbal preparations in traditional medicine. The preparation often has a common effect of reducing triglycerides and LDL cholesterol levels. It is made from the roots and seeds of the Commiphora mukul tree, which are rich in guggulsterone, a compound shown to have anti-inflammatory and lipid-lowering properties. The process of making Commiphora mukul tincture is moderately difficult, requiring careful extraction of the bioactive compounds from the roots and seeds, followed by solvent-based extraction and concentration.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh or dried roots and seeds of Commiphora mukul and clean them thoroughly.
Dry the roots and seeds further if they are not already dry, and then chop them into small pieces.
Combine the chopped roots and seeds in a clean glass jar and add a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin to cover them completely.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for several weeks, shaking the jar periodically to facilitate extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, and discard the solids.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Decoction made from this plant is used to treat hyperlipidemia, atherosclerosis and associated cardiovascular diseases due to its hypocholesterolemic and antioxidant properties.
preparations.preparation_overview: Commiphora mukul decoction is a traditional herbal remedy used to treat various health conditions, including hyperlipidemia, diabetes, hypertension, arthritis, gastritis, liver cirrhosis, and pneumonia. It's a common preparation, widely used in Ayurvedic medicine due to its availability and effectiveness. The decoction has a common effect of reducing inflammation and improving overall health. Commiphora mukul decoction is made from the roots and seeds of the Commiphora mukul tree, which are rich in guggulsterones and other bioactive compounds. The preparation requires some expertise and is moderately difficult to make, but its benefits have made it a staple in traditional medicine.
preparation_recipe: Gather and clean the roots of Commiphora mukul and remove any debris or impurities.
Dry the roots in a shaded area or using a food dehydrator to preserve their potency.
Roast the dried roots and seeds of Commiphora mukul in a pan over low heat until fragrant.
Combine the roasted roots and seeds with water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat and simmer the mixture for an extended period to extract the herbal properties.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Commiphora mukul are experiencing hepatotoxicity due to its potential to cause liver damage, developing gastrointestinal disturbances such as abdominal pain, and inducing hemorrhagic diarrhea, a condition characterized by severe and bloody diarrhea. Additionally, improperly using Commiphora mukul can cause renal failure, a life-threatening condition where the kidneys lose their ability to function, and lead to photosensitization, a condition where the skin becomes sensitive to sunlight. Furthermore, it can induce thrombocytopenia, a condition where the platelet count is abnormally low, and cause nausea and vomiting, which can lead to dehydration and electrolyte imbalances. Gastrointestinal disturbances can also progress to severe conditions like gastrointestinal bleeding, and in some cases, cardiovascular issues may arise due to the plant's potential to interact with other medications. Cardiovascular issues can be further exacerbated by the development of thrombocytopenia and other complications, emphasizing the importance of proper use.

precautions.precaution_list_description: Pregnant or breastfeeding women should consult a doctor before using Commiphora mukul due to potential hormonal effects.
Regularly check liver enzymes due to Commiphora mukul's potential hepatotoxicity.
Commiphora mukul is contraindicated in pregnant and breastfeeding women due to potential harm to the fetus or infant.
May lower blood sugar levels in patients with diabetes, potentially requiring dose adjustments of diabetes medications.
Exceeding the recommended dose of Commiphora mukul may cause adverse effects.
Commiphora mukul guggul may interact with diabetes medications, blood thinners, and medications for high blood pressure and heart conditions.

precautions.precaution_name: Avoid in kidney or liver disease
precautions.precaution_list_description: Commiphora mukul is contraindicated in patients with kidney or liver disease due to potential exacerbation of conditions.
Commiphora mukul oil can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals.

precautions.precaution_name: Do not use long-term without guidance
precautions.precaution_list_description: Prolonged use without medical supervision can lead to adverse effects and interact with other medications.
Inform your healthcare provider about any adverse reactions or allergic reactions to Commiphora mukul.

intro_study: According to a study published by "Cardiovascular & hematological agents in medicinal chemistry", Commiphora mukul has been found to possess anti-arthritic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate joint pain and inflammation. Additionally, it has been reported to have anti-hypercholesterolemic and anti-hyperlipidemic properties, suggesting its potential in reducing cholesterol and lipid levels in the blood. Furthermore, Commiphora mukul has been shown to have antioxidant and cardioprotective effects, which can help protect the heart and reduce the risk of cardiovascular diseases.

According to a study published by "The international journal of lower extremity wounds", the application of Commiphora mukul oleo gum resin significantly accelerated wound healing in rats by reducing the wound area and improving skin elasticity compared to the control groups. Furthermore, Commiphora mukul gum extract increased the levels of vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF), which plays a crucial role in promoting wound healing. These findings suggest that Commiphora mukul has potential wound healing properties, indicating its potential use in treating skin wounds.
properties_description: The therapeutic properties of Commiphora mukul are attributed to its antimicrobial activity, which prevents the growth of microorganisms and reduces the risk of infections. This plant's antioxidant properties help protect cells from oxidative damage, while its anti-inflammatory effects alleviate pain and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. The analgesic properties of Commiphora mukul provide relief from pain, which is further enhanced by its ability to reduce pain perception. Additionally, Commiphora mukul has been found to possess hypolipidemic properties, which help regulate lipid levels and prevent conditions such as atherosclerosis, and it also exhibits hepatoprotective effects by protecting the liver from damage and promoting its function. Furthermore, the plant's antidiabetic properties help regulate blood sugar levels, and its cardioprotective effects help prevent cardiovascular diseases by reducing blood pressure and improving lipid profiles.
constituents_description: The healing constituents of Commiphora mukul are a complex mix of bioactive compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties. Saponins, primarily guggulsterones, have been shown to inhibit HMG-CoA reductase, a key enzyme in cholesterol synthesis, thereby lowering lipid profiles and exhibiting anti-hyperlipidemic effects. Flavonoids, including kaempferol and quercetin, possess antioxidant properties that protect against oxidative stress and inflammation. The sterol fraction of Commiphora mukul contains guggulsterol I and II, which have been demonstrated to exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory activities. Additionally, terpenoids, such as guggulol and sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, including ferulic and caffeic acids, also contribute to the overall therapeutic potential of Commiphora mukul.
preparation_description: The most common herbal preparation of Commiphora mukul for medicinal purposes are capsules, which are used to manage cholesterol levels and reduce inflammation. Commiphora mukul oil, obtained through steam distillation of the plant's resin, is primarily employed as a topical treatment for skin conditions such as eczema and acne. A tincture of Commiphora mukul, typically made from the plant's resin, is used to treat various health issues, including fever and rheumatism. Additionally, a decoction of the plant's bark and roots is utilized as a digestive aid to alleviate symptoms of indigestion and diarrhea. The oil is also used as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent.
precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Commiphora mukul medicinally are to consult a doctor before use, especially if you have a medical history or are taking medications, to determine the safe dosage for your condition. It is also crucial to monitor liver function, as Commiphora mukul may cause liver damage in some individuals. Additionally, pregnant and breastfeeding women should avoid using Commiphora mukul due to the potential risks to the fetus or baby. People with diabetes should use Commiphora mukul with caution, as it may lower blood sugar levels. Other precautions include not exceeding the recommended dose, being aware of potential interactions with other medications, avoiding use in individuals with kidney or liver disease, and reporting any side effects, such as skin rashes, to a healthcare provider, and also being cautious not to use it long-term without guidance.
intro_description: Commiphora mukul, commonly known as guggul, is a medicinal herb that has been extensively used to treat various health conditions, including hyperlipidemia, obesity, and cardiovascular diseases. The main health benefits of Commiphora mukul include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce body weight, and improve lipid profiles. The herb is also valued for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The main bioactive compounds responsible for the medicinal properties of Commiphora mukul include guggulsterone, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and lipid-lowering effects. Guggul is often used to make herbal preparations, such as guggul powder, which can be mixed with water or used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
